# Verify Waitlist and Scarcity Manipulation

## Claim being tested

Scarcity/waitlist numbers represent real demand.

## Alternative hypothesis

The waitlist or scarcity count is inflated, recycled, unverifiable, or used as social proof without real conversion.

## Data fields

- waitlist count timestamp
- unique verified users
- duplicate emails/devices
- invitation waves
- conversion to active users
- retention
- deletion/unsubscribe rate
- public count changes

## Metrics

- waitlist-to-activation ratio
- duplicate rate
- count growth discontinuities
- invite-wave conversion quality
- retention by signup cohort

## Safe conclusion

"Scarcity signal should be discounted where public waitlist counts are not tied to verified unique users and downstream activation."
